title: Hierarchical Summarization with Contrastive Planning
abstract: We study abstractive text summarization with a contrastive planning module that first predicts sentence-level content plans and then generates summaries. Experiments on CNN/DailyMail report stronger ROUGE-L than a Transformer baseline.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Biomedical Question Answering with Graph Memory
abstract: This paper proposes a retrieval-augmented method for biomedical question answering. The method combines dense retrieval with a graph memory module and is evaluated on PubMedQA using accuracy and F1.

title: Instruction-Tuned Vision-Language Models for Chart Understanding
abstract: We propose an instruction-tuned multimodal method for chart question answering. The model is tested on ChartQA and PlotQA and achieves improved exact match over prior vision-language baselines.

title: Benchmarking Robust NER under Noisy User Text
abstract: We study named entity recognition on noisy user-generated text and evaluate several encoder architectures on WNUT 2017. The benchmark uses F1 as the primary metric and highlights the gains from data augmentation.

title: Adaptive Prompt Routing for Multi-Domain Dialogue Systems
abstract: We propose a prompt-routing method for task-oriented dialogue that selects domain-specific prompts at inference time. Evaluations on MultiWOZ show higher success rate and BLEU than static prompting.

title: Low-Resource Machine Translation with Synthetic Curriculum Data
abstract: This work studies low-resource machine translation and proposes a synthetic curriculum learning strategy. Tests on Flores report better BLEU for Nepali-English and Sinhala-English translation.

title: Measuring Faithfulness in Long-Form Question Answering
abstract: We study faithfulness evaluation for long-form question answering and introduce a metric suite that combines entailment checks with citation coverage. Experiments on ELI5 report improved correlation with human judgments.

title: Code Search with Structure-Aware Dual Encoders
abstract: We propose a structure-aware dual-encoder method for code search over large repositories. The model is evaluated on CodeSearchNet and improves MRR while maintaining efficient retrieval latency.
